items.headline: Mbappé vs. Haaland, winner takes the group — France play Norway today
items.body: The heavyweight clash of the group stage. France and Norway are both already through, so this Group I finale (3pm ET, Boston) is a straight shootout for top spot — and a Golden Boot duel. Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland have four goals each through two games, chasing Messi (5) for the tournament lead.
items.say: France-Norway is on today — basically Mbappé vs. Haaland for the group and the Golden Boot. That's a heavyweight matchup; you watching?

links.text: Olympics.com: France v Norway live & Golden Boot race
FOX Sports: how to watch Norway vs. France

items.headline: USMNT lose a wild one to Türkiye — but they're already in the knockouts
items.body: The U.S. had top spot locked, so Pochettino made 10 changes and rested Pulisic — and still went toe-to-toe. Auston Trusty struck inside three minutes and Sebastian Berhalter made it 2-2, before Kaan Ayhan grabbed a heartbreaking winner deep in stoppage time for a 3-2 Türkiye win. Next up: Bosnia & Herzegovina in the Round of 32 on July 1.
items.say: USMNT lost a crazy one to Türkiye 3-2 on a last-second goal, but they'd already qualified — coach rested half the team. Knockouts start July 1.

links.text: FOX Sports: Türkiye vs. USA extended highlights
CNN: late winner gives USA its first defeat

items.headline: Falcons sweep FURIA to win their first-ever CS2 Major
items.body: Curtains on the IEM Cologne Major: Team Falcons blanked Brazil's FURIA 3-0 in the grand final at a roaring Lanxess Arena to lift their first Major trophy. Russian phenom Ilya 'm0NESY' Osipov was named MVP — the crown jewel on a $1.25M event and a breakout moment for the squad.
items.say: Falcons just won their first Counter-Strike Major — swept FURIA 3-0 in Cologne and m0NESY took MVP. Big moment for that roster.

links.text: ESL: Falcons vs. FURIA grand final highlights
esports.gg: how Falcons broke the curse

items.headline: Wizards make AJ Dybantsa the No. 1 pick
items.body: Washington kicked off the NBA Draft by taking BYU's AJ Dybantsa first overall. The 6-foot-9 wing averaged 25.5 points a game in his lone college season — and became the first player to lead Division I in scoring and go No. 1 since Glenn Robinson in 1994. A new face to build around in D.C.
items.say: The Wizards took AJ Dybantsa No. 1 in the draft — leading scorer in college ball, first guy to do that and go first overall since 1994. Big swing for D.C.

links.text: NBA: AJ Dybantsa's No. 1 pick press conference

items.headline: Wimbledon draw is out — play starts Monday
items.body: Grass-court season hits its peak: the Championships begin June 29 at the All England Club, and today's draw set the bracket. Seven-time champ Novak Djokovic landed in world No. 1 Jannik Sinner's half, and Taylor Fritz drew Jack Draper in a blockbuster first-rounder. Strawberries and cream incoming.
items.say: Wimbledon starts Monday and the draw just dropped — Djokovic's in Sinner's half already. Always a fun two weeks. You follow it?

links.text: WTA: Wimbledon 2026 dates, draw & what to know

items.headline: 'Supergirl' flies into theaters today
items.body: Milly Alcock takes the cape after her scene-stealing cameo in last year's 'Superman.' DC Studios' new film sends Kara Zor-El on a revenge-fueled road trip across the galaxy — Jason Momoa's bounty hunter Lobo and a dog named Krypto in tow. It's out now, including IMAX.
items.say: The Supergirl movie's finally out — Milly Alcock's got the cape. Could be the weekend pick if you're in the mood for a superhero road trip.

items.headline: 'Jackass: Best and Last' is the franchise's final hurrah
items.body: Twenty-five years on, Knoxville, Steve-O and the crew take a victory lap. The fifth and final Jackass film mixes greatest-hits stunts with brand-new gags and never-before-seen footage — a 'dumbly fitting' goodbye, per the reviews. In theaters today, ice packs optional.
items.say: There's a final Jackass movie out today — 25-year sendoff, old classics plus new stunts. Honestly might be the most fun you have at the theater this weekend.

links.text: Jackass: Best and Last — official site

items.headline: Larry David's back — 'Life, Larry and the Pursuit of Unhappiness' premieres
items.body: Call it 'Curb Your Enthusiasm in costumes.' Larry David's new HBO Max comedy drops Kvetching Larry into American history — the Boston Tea Party, the Wright brothers, V-J Day — across improvised sketches. It's produced with the Obamas' Higher Ground, with Seinfeld, Jon Hamm and Bill Hader popping up. First episode is up now.
items.say: Larry David's new show just premiered — it's basically Curb, but set throughout American history. Sketches with Seinfeld, Bill Hader, the works. Very up his alley.

links.text: NPR: the Obamas team up with Larry David

items.headline: Katy Perry torches her past on new single 'Watch It Burn'
items.body: It's a busy New Music Friday — Muse, Justin Bieber and Tyler, the Creator all in the mix — but Katy Perry's comeback is the talker. 'Watch It Burn' continues the storyline from her single 'Bandaids,' complete with a cinematic video where she sprouts a scorpion tail and sets her old life ablaze. Her next pop era is officially lit.
items.say: Katy Perry dropped a new single, 'Watch It Burn' — she literally grows a scorpion tail in the video. Feels like the start of a whole new era for her.
